explanations.5: This tells the system not to ask interactive questions during package installation, letting the Docker build run automatically without waiting for user input.
explanations.12: The --mount flags create temporary storage areas during this single build step: one for caching apt packages to speed up rebuilds, and one for temporary log files that won't be saved in the final image.
explanations.16: The install command creates directories with specific permissions (0755 means readable/executable by everyone), and > /dev/null hides the output so it doesn't clutter the build logs.
explanations.18: This downloads Mozilla's GPG key (used to verify that packages are authentic) and pipes it to the next command; the pipe symbol | means 'send this output as input to the next command'.
explanations.19: The gpg command converts the key from text format into binary format and saves it to a file where apt can find and use it to verify Firefox packages.
explanations.21: This creates an apt source entry that tells the package manager where to download Firefox from, including which GPT key to use to verify it's legitimate.
explanations.24: This file tells apt to strongly prefer Firefox packages from Mozilla's repository over any other source, using a priority score of 1000.
explanations.27: The -qq flags make apt extra quiet (only show errors), and DEBIAN_FRONTEND is set again here to ensure no interactive prompts happen during the install.
explanations.30: This tells Docker that when someone runs this container, the default action is to start Firefox.
